name: Kurentovanje
dates: Eleven days before Ash Wednesday (February/March), culminating on Shrove Tuesday (Fat Tuesday). The Ptuj Kurentovanje typically runs over two weekends.
description: The most famous and most-attended carnival in Slovenia, centered on the city of Ptuj — Slovenia's oldest city. The central figure is the Kurent (plural: Kurenti), a traditional masquerade character wearing a sheepskin costume, a tall feathered or horned hat, a chain of large cowbells around the waist, and a club or chain. Kurent processions march through the streets driving away winter and evil spirits with the clanging of their bells. The Kurent is considered one of the most distinct ethnographic figures in Slovene folk tradition and is a powerful symbol of Slovenian cultural identity. The festival attracts tens of thousands of visitors annually. UNESCO recognised the Kurentovanje as Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity. Beyond Ptuj, Kurent groups are active in many Slovenian villages in the Ptujsko polje (Ptuj plain) area, and the custom appears in Slovenian communities in other countries.
